steps.title: Determine the slope's sign
steps.content: The scatterplot's $y$-values rise as $x$ increases, climbing from about $(1, 25)$ to $(5, 65)$. The slope must be positive, and approximately $$\frac{65 - 25}{5 - 1} = 10.$$

steps.title: Pin down the y-intercept with a data point
steps.content:
Test $x = 1, y \approx 25$ in each choice:
  • Choice A ($14.7 - 10x$): $14.7 - 10 = 4.7$ — wrong slope sign, doesn't match.
  • Choice B ($14.7 + 10x$): $14.7 + 10 = 24.7$ — matches the data.
  • Choice C ($-14.7 - 10x$): $-24.7$ — far off.
  • Choice D ($-14.7 + 10x$): $-4.7$ — nowhere near $25$.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
